reasoning: The value for 2025-11-09 defaults to the most recent trading day, November 7, which was 3.49%. This 3.49% reflects a late-week dip from a very recent plateau of ~3.55%. The primary anchor, provided by the FRED statistical trend model and seasonal analysis, suggests a combined 0.61 probability of an increase above 3.49% by mid-November, alongside a linear trend projection of 3.502%. Using this as a strong prior and factoring in short-term mean reversion, I place the probability of an increase by November 16 at roughly 58%. In accordance with instructions to progressively increase uncertainty (convergence to 0.5) for more distant horizons, I decrease the probability toward 50% for the subsequent dates, assigning 55% for December 09 (just before the next FOMC announcement) and 52% for February 07, given the broader backdrop of a cautious Fed easing cycle where rates could gradually drift lower over the medium-term despite short-term fluctuations.
